title: Support for Unicode in TXT Connector
breadcrumbs: Support for Unicode in TXT Connector
text: Support for Unicode in TXT Connector The TXT U2.0 connector supports Unicode. It is possible to specify different types of Unicode encoding in the same application. Uniface provides the following mechanisms for using Unicode for data handed by the TXT connector: Use the W (wide-character) packing code for all fields in the entity definition. For an existing application that currently uses ASCII text files, you can switch to a default Unicode encoding of the TXT connector by setting the uni connector option for the TXT connector. To override the default encoding for selected entities, you can add the /uni switch to the entity's path setting. You can set Unicode as the default character set with $DEF_CHARSET . For more information, see uni and $def_charset . Related Links Unicode Unicode is an industry standard designed to allow text and symbols from all languages to be consistently represented and manipulated by computers. Unicode in Uniface Applications Unicode characters can be used in fields, variables, parameters, and the user interface, but there are some restrictions. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
